title: Connect Item and Subitem in different boards
excerpt: In board A, I have a list of speakers (name, title, company, head shot etc…) . In board B, I have a list of events (London, Paris, Rome etc …). Under each event, I have speakers as sub items, these are the same individua…

posts.author: LeanneM (Leanne McConnachie)
posts.cookedHtml:
In board A, I have a list of speakers (name, title, company, head shot etc…) . In board B, I have a list of events (London, Paris, Rome etc …). Under each event, I have speakers as sub items, these are the same individuals as those on board A.

What I want to do is maintain a database of speakers that will serve as the single source of truth while also connecting it to the sub-items beneath each event in board B.

I am using Super Forms in board A to enable speakers to update their own information as necessary.

Speakers will update information such as presentation titles or headshots. This item needs to be linked from board A as the database to board B the events, where a connection is established between the sub-item in B and the item in A as they are the same person.

Does this make sense? Is anyone doing anything similar?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: That sounds like it should work perfectly, are you running into an issue?

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Leanne, you will just need to connect and mirror the information from Board A on Board B. So create your Connect, and Mirror Columns on Board/s B. Unfortunately matching automations (that automatically connect items across boards based on a value such as ‘Name’ of the item) don’t work with subitems yet, but if you are open to using apps then subitem automations may have what you need.

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org
